title: ajuga vulgaris
intro: Ajuga vulgaris, also known as bugleweed, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the mint family. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of Ajuga vulgaris have been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments due to their antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as lowering blood pressure, reducing anxiety and stress, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Ajuga vulgaris is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can also be consumed fresh or dried to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
